title: Beehive Apiary: Forager Dispatch under Patch Saturation
seed_problem: Assign drones to delivery zones under per-drone capacity to maximize served weight
mutation: composite: replace the anchor's per-agent capacity + additive value with a per-resource SATURATION model -- each patch has a depletable nectar stock S_j and the harvest from a patch is min(S_j, total appetite dispatched there); foragers reach only a sparse set of patches and are assigned at most one. The objective sum_j min(S_j, A_j) is submodular/concave, making over-crowding wasteful; the true optimum is NP-hard and value-blind first-fit, saturation-aware marginal-gain greedy, and reassignment local search diverge.
strategies: reference first-fit: send every forager to its lowest-indexed reachable patch (concentrates load, saturates low patches; ratio ~0.1)
saturation-aware greedy: process foragers by descending best appetite, send each to the reachable patch maximizing marginal gain clamp(S_j - load_j, 0, a) given current loads
greedy + reassignment local search: from the greedy dispatch, repeatedly move each forager to the reachable patch giving the best net (add - remove) marginal change until no improvement
knapsack/spreading construction: treat each patch as a saturating bucket and rebalance foragers off over-saturated patches toward under-used reachable patches
